Patch Set 1: Verified-1

This fails in the (normally disabled for review runs) sanity test 71.

Apparently we cannot just drop page_writeback from prep_page_write but need to make it conditional on whenever the IO is sync or async (e.g. coming from ll_writepage where the kernel dropped the dirty bit for us).

 <4>[34478.338585] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity test 71: Running dbench on lustre (don't segment fault) ====== 04:33:00 (1437121980)
 <3>[34483.779415] LustreError: 3775:0:(llite_lib.c:1647:ll_setattr_raw()) [0x200002341:0xa3:0x0]HSM set dirty failed: rc2 = -22
 <0>[34483.957734] LustreError: 1701:0:(vvp_page.c:296:vvp_page_completion_write()) ASSERTION( PageWriteback(vmpage) ) failed:

parents.subject: Revert "LU-8922 lod: check master stripes properly"

commit.subject: LU-6854 llite: Do not set writeback for sync write pages
commit.message:
LU-6854 llite: Do not set writeback for sync write pages

Sync-write pages don't have Dirty bit set, it will confuse newer
kernels by having writeback bit set.

Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I2613919a9e942a7ee3dc48b250f7f0e5e4594e48
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/15610
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
